questionText: Simplify $\dfrac{\sec^2\theta - 1}{\sec^2\theta}$, giving your answer as a single trigonometric function squared.

steps.description: Replace the numerator using an identity
steps.explanation: The identity $\sec^2\theta = 1 + \tan^2\theta$ rearranges so the numerator becomes $\tan^2\theta$.
Substituting simplifies the numerator, leaving a ratio of two squared functions.

steps.description: Write tangent and secant in sine and cosine
steps.explanation: Converting both to sine and cosine makes the cancellation visible.

steps.description: Divide by multiplying by the reciprocal
steps.explanation: Dividing by $\frac{1}{\cos^2\theta}$ is multiplying by $\cos^2\theta$, which will cancel the denominator.
The expression reduces to $\sin^2\theta$, a single trigonometric function squared as required.
